headline: Meryl Streep's Miranda Priestly Returns: Fashion's Most Iconic Boss Refuses to Mellow
lede: Fifteen years after her career-defining turn as Miranda Priestly, Meryl Streep proves her razor-sharp character remains as cutting-edge as ever in the unexpected sequel 'The Devil Wears Prada 2'.
body:
The fashion world trembles once again as Meryl Streep resurrects her legendary *Runway* magazine editrix, demonstrating that some characters are truly timeless. Nearly two decades after the original film's cultural watershed moment, Streep's Miranda Priestly continues to command attention with the same icy precision that made her a pop culture icon.

Where lesser performers might soften with age, Streep doubles down on the character's sardonic brilliance. Her recent media tour suggests the sequel will maintain the razor-sharp wit that defined the original, proving her understanding of power dynamics in media remains unparalleled.

Industry watchers note the strategic genius of revisiting this character. The original film transformed how Hollywood viewed fashion industry narratives, elevating what could have been a lightweight comedy into a nuanced exploration of ambition and professional identity.

The film's return arrives at a critical moment for representation of professional women on screen. Miranda Priestly remains a complex antihero — demanding, brilliant, unapologetically ambitious — who refuses to conform to traditional likability tropes.
key_facts: Sequel comes 15 years after original film
Meryl Streep reprises iconic Miranda Priestly role
Original film grossed over $326 million worldwide
Directed by David Frankel, same as original
